textPlain: TangentI had an rc submarine that could go underwater a couple feet, but I'd take an rc car's 27MHz radio and put it underwater, it'd stop working almost immediately soon as it went underwater (waterproofed). Wonder what the difference was.
Software defined radio but what is LTR?
It is likely that that the sub had it's antenna tuned to work well in water while the RC car antenna was tuned for open air. The two different mediums will change the antenna impedance.

The original low cost SDR was a European TV tuner USB stick.  A driver developer noticed that it was possible to turn off the built-in vertical and horizontal blank suppression to get a raw I/Q dump from a device that was available for $20 retail.  This revolutionized the hobbyist SDR community as the purpose built devices cost hundreds or thousands of dollars.The "RTL" came from the company that built the hackable chip: Realtek.
I believe the RTL in RTL-SDR is "Realtek Limited", the manufacturer of the chips used in the early days of SDR. I don't think the chips these days are exclusively Realtek, but the name has persisted.
